English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Alan Rabinowitz papers
Rabinowitz, Alan.
Arthur D. Little, Inc
6708
2.9 cubic feet. 2.9 cubic feet.
1960-2017. Printed Materials, Printed Materials, Publications, Reports Reports and documents relating to his work in planning, housing, urban renewal, municipal finance and urban economics, which integrate the worlds of public planning, public finance, and private development. Collection material in English 5789042
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Julia Parker
Date completed:
April 20, 2009
EAD encoding:
Julia Parker, April 20, 2009
Updated:
Jude Corina, January 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
City planner. Member Lambda Alpha International, New York Chapter (1950s) and Puget Sound Chapter.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Reports and documents relating to his work in planning, housing, urban renewal, municipal finance and urban economics, which integrate the worlds of public planning, public finance, and private development.
Report on "Non-planning and Redevelopment in Boston," 1972; two bound volumes of reports on planning and land development in Florida, Missouri, California, and Chicago written while on the staff of Arthur D. Little, Inc., Cambridge, MA. and other Arthur D. Little reports, 1960-1963; bound volume: "Industrial Zoning in Seattle, 1974;" bound volume, "Management of Tourism in Massachusetts," 1965. Also, M.I.T. dissertation, "Municipal Debt/Intergovernmental Fiscal Relations," 1969; "An Alternate Plan for Cooper Square," 1961; various Lambda Alpha publications; and selective bibliography and work history, 2006.
Materials about the Cambridge Institute, its New City Project, and its related Center for Community Economic Development.
Additional books, consulting reports and organizational materials, writings on historical underpinnings of city and regional planning, readings on progressive planning, professional papers and studies on urban planning/economics topics.
Research reports used for The Real Estate Gamble and Municipal Data Projection Project reports.
"Clara's Town: A Passion Play in the Years 1908-1910," an account by Alan Rabinowitz, with diary entries, relating to his mother Clara Greenhut. "Alan's Iconography of New York," 1914: an illustrated tour of places where members of their families (Rabinowitz and Wolf) have lived in Manhattan; places that are featured in the biography of Aaron Rabinowitz; including his connection to Fred F. French; and other connections to French's family and his companies.
RELATED MATERIALS
Related collections: Walter Thabit papers, #4215 http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMM04215.
NOTES
Part of the Lambda Alpha Literary Archives.
Cite As:
Alan Rabinowitz papers, #6708. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
New York (N.Y.) -- Description and travel. City planners. Tourism -- Massachusetts. Zoning -- Washington -- Seattle. City planning -- Washington -- Seattle. City planning -- Illinois -- Chicago. City planning -- California. City planning -- Missouri -- Kansas City. City planning -- New York (N.Y) City planning -- Florida. City planning -- Massachusetts -- Boston. Thabit, Walter, 1921-2005 Greenhut, Clara. Rabinowitz, Aaron, 1884-1973. French, Fred F. (Fred Fillmore), 1883-1936 Rabinowitz family. Wolf family. Lambda Alpha International Cambridge Institute (Cambridge, Mass.) Center for Community Economic Development Planners for Equal Opportunity
